     The Numbers Business Report

Sky Captain and the World of Tomorrow (2004)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $37,760,080Details
International Box Office $11,970,774
Worldwide Box Office $49,730,854
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Sky Captain and reporter Polly Perkins investigate the disappearance of well-known scientists around the world. Together with Frankly Cook, commander of an all-female amphibious squadron, and technical genius Dex they try to stop Dr. Totenkopf from executing his evil plot to destroy the world.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$15,580,278 (41.3% of total gross)
Legs:2.42 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:75.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$70,000,000 (worldwide box office is 0.7 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,170 opening theaters/3,170 max. theaters, 4.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $72,723,115

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 17th, 2004 (Wide) by Paramount Pictures
International Releases: February 4th, 2005 (Wide), released as Sky Captain And The World Of Tomorrow (Australia)
Video Release: April 18th, 2006 by Paramount Home Video, released as Sky Captain & the World of Tomorrow
MPA Rating: PG for sequences of stylized sci-fi violence and brief mild language
Running Time: 107 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Visual Effects
Geographical setting: New York, New York City
Plot point: Investigative Journalist, Mad Scientist, Missing Person, Robot
Subgenre: Action Adventure
Time period setting: 1930s, Steampunk
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Animation/Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Brooklyn Films II, Riff Raff, Blue Flower, Filmauro
Production Countries: United States, United Kingdom, Italy
Languages: English
